_comment: Gateway_v0.21 wallowing-hole (mud pool) coordinate data imported from wiredredman's Steam community guide 'Locations And POI's (GATEWAY)'. Cross-verified against two mirrors (gameplay.tips and bisecthosting.com) — all three sources agree on coord values. Author publishes coords in Unreal cm, same format the isle-overlay clipboard watcher captures. Z omitted from the emitted records here because pois.json uses (x, y) only; Z values retained in `_source_z` for optional future use. The guide contains 24 distinct wallowing-hole entries for Gateway; 'Vein Island South' shares byte-identical coords with 'Vein Island North' in the source text (a guide-author copy-paste error, faithfully preserved in mirrors), so only 23 unique coordinate points exist and Vein Island South is flagged `duplicate_coord_of_north: true`. No 'mud_pools' category exists as a distinct in-game feature — per evrimaquickguide.com/gameplay/health-statuses/wallowing, 'mud pool' and 'wallowing hole' are the same interactive object; Vulnona's mud_pools category is empty across Gateway_v0.21 data_1..data_4 for this reason.

source_attribution.primary: wiredredman, Steam Community Guide 'Locations And POI's (GATEWAY)', https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3065862163 (Wallowing Holes section), verbatim coord strings; verified 2026-04-16
source_attribution.cross_check_1: gameplay.tips mirror of the same guide, https://gameplay.tips/guides/the-isle-locations-and-pois-gateway-map.html (Wallowing Holes section) — coords match source 1:1
source_attribution.cross_check_2: bisecthosting.com/blog/the-isle-maps-locations-gateway-thenyaw-test-level — 22 wallow coords listed (omits Island Wallowing Hole and Vein Island South in its summary), those 22 match source exactly
source_attribution.mechanics_reference: https://www.evrimaquickguide.com/gameplay/health-statuses/wallowing — confirms 'wallow' and 'mud pool' refer to the same mechanic

coordinate_system: Unreal cm (east = +x, north historically = +y but Evrima uses +y = north positive per our map transform; matches isle-overlay clipboard watcher format)
naming_note: Entries preserve the guide author's names (wiredredman's terminology) rather than remapping to the 23 placeholder names currently in pois.json. Placeholder names like 'Thunderdome Wallow', 'Central Plains Wallow', 'Jungle Wallow North/South', 'Coastal Jungle Wallow', 'Hidden Dam Wallow', 'Swamp Sanctuary Wallow', 'Delta Channel Wallow', 'River Delta Wallow West', and 'Big Island Pond Wallow' have no confidently-matching entry in wiredredman's guide; do NOT fabricate a mapping. The overlay maintainer should decide remapping case-by-case based on in-game verification or leave placeholders as-is.

exact_or_high_confidence.guide_entry: Plains Wallowing Hole (description: 'Next to the plains pond')
Vein Island North + Vein Island Mid (two entries, guide lists them separately)

possible_but_ambiguous_DO_NOT_auto_map.reason: guide says 'island very close to mainland' but coord (-334275, -272587) is far southwest of the Plains biome; may or may not be the same feature as the placeholder named 'Big Island Pond'
both are sanctuary-adjacent but names differ; Access Sanctuary is at Water Access, not the Hidden biome

NAME COLLISION — guide's 'Twin Wallowing Holes' is described as 'closeby to the hidden pond and highlands', NOT near the Twin Bridges landmark. Do NOT auto-map; these are almost certainly different features.

wallows.source: wiredredman Steam guide — SOURCE TYPO: coord string identical to Vein Island North in the original guide text. Verified on multiple mirrors (Steam + gameplay.tips) — this is the guide author's copy-paste error, not a fetch artifact. Actual south-island coords are unknown; overlay maintainer should verify in-game before shipping this point. Description: 'south end of vein island near a salt lick'

mud_pools.explanation: No source publishes a separate 'mud pool' category for Gateway. Vulnona's menu has a mud_pools entry but data_1..data_4.txt for Gateway_v0.21 contain zero entries. Per evrimaquickguide.com/gameplay/health-statuses/wallowing, 'mud pool' is the same in-game interactive object as 'wallowing hole' / 'wallow'. All 24 entries above double as the mud-pool dataset.
